operations.reason: OIDC callback is a browser redirect target handled by the IdP round-trip, so the frontend intentionally does not call it through a runtime wrapper.
The web app subscribes only to project-scoped streams; the global platform stream is currently exposed for non-frontend consumers.
The web Project AI runtime now consumes the project-level mux stream, while the legacy single-conversation stream remains available for compatibility, CLI, and debugging flows.
Ticket detail currently renders hook history from the detail payload and does not maintain a dedicated live hook stream subscription.
The onboarding/bootstrap flow still resolves builtin role templates from the list payload, so the new single-role endpoint is intentionally not wired into the current frontend yet.
The ticket UI uses the project-scoped detail endpoint instead of the standalone ticket fetch route.
Ticket comments are loaded through the ticket detail payload and comment mutation flows, not a standalone comments list request.
The frontend has no agent deletion affordance yet, so the generated wrapper is intentionally unused.
Agent screens currently load list payloads rather than a dedicated single-agent detail call.
The workspace refresh removed the old agent output sheet, so the list wrapper is intentionally unused until a replacement browser surface is wired.
The old agent output sheet was removed in the workspace refresh, leaving the live output stream exposed only for non-frontend consumers until a new UI binds it.
The workspace refresh removed the old agent step inspector, so the list wrapper is intentionally unused until a replacement browser surface is wired.
The old agent step inspector was removed in the workspace refresh, leaving the live step stream exposed only for non-frontend consumers until a new UI binds it.
Machine management currently relies on list, resources, test, and health refresh endpoints instead of a standalone machine detail fetch.
The machines UI currently refreshes through snapshot polling and targeted mutations rather than maintaining an organization-wide machine event stream subscription.
Provider detail fetch was added for CLI/operator flows and is intentionally not wired into the current frontend screens.
Project token usage is queryable for backend and operator flows, and the generated frontend wrapper exists, but the current browser UI does not yet expose a project-scoped token analytics surface.
Runtime secret resolution is a backend control-plane capability and remains intentionally unbound in the browser until the later secret management UI work lands.

Scoped secret foundation endpoints ship before the dedicated security settings UI, so the backend contract is intentionally available for runtime and follow-on integration work before browser screens bind it.
Project pages use summary/list/update flows and do not currently fetch a standalone project detail payload.
Workspace bootstrap currently restores organizations through app-context and server-provided session state instead of the legacy standalone organizations wrapper.
The current app shell derives project navigation from existing summary/bootstrap flows, so this older standalone organization-projects wrapper remains intentionally unused.
Skill refresh targets the active agent workspace and is triggered by CLI/runtime flows rather than the browser settings page.
Repository settings derive mirror readiness from repo list data and mirror mutation responses rather than a dedicated mirror list fetch.
Project update revision history is preserved and queryable in the backend for auditability, but the current Updates UI does not expose a revision browser yet.
Project update comment revision history is preserved and queryable in the backend for auditability, but the current Updates UI does not expose a revision browser yet.
Repository settings expose prepare and sync mirror actions, but there is no separate verify action in the current UI.
Ticket detail embeds repo scopes in the detail payload, so the standalone repo-scope list wrapper is intentionally unused.
Workflow screens load list and harness payloads and do not currently issue a standalone workflow detail request.
